Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 10:40 ET
Extreme P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion trade into FOMC week
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts (1.53 P/C) combined with single-name calls (0.70 P/C) indicates large-scale dispersion hedging ahead of mega-cap earnings and FOMC.
  • 2. Positive gamma environment dampens moves, but 5 names in vol backwardation suggest pockets of elevated event risk.
  • 3. Unusual vol spikes in LH, TRVI, and HAS appear merger/deal-related rather than informed positioning.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge mode

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence — index puts trading 1.53x calls while single-names show call-heavy 0.70x ratios, a 0.83 spread indicating institutional dispersion positioning. This structure typically emerges when funds hedge broad exposure while maintaining single-name conviction into binary events, particularly relevant with MSFT, META, GOOG, AMZN, and AAPL all reporting within 5 days alongside the FOMC decision. The $628M positive SPY gamma provides a technical backstop that should dampen volatility, but the term structure shows 2.2pts of contango suggesting markets are pricing elevated risk beyond the immediate event cluster. Single-name anomalies require careful parsing between news-driven and positioning-driven flows. LH's explosive +26,793% put spike alongside +12,757% call volume suggests M&A speculation rather than informed bearish positioning, while OKLO's +939% call surge continues the nuclear theme following recent sector momentum. More telling is the vol surface divergence — TXN and ADI trading cheap vol with rich skew, while semiconductor leaders like NVDA and AMD show backwardation despite technical strength scores above 90. The institutional 13F layer adds critical context to current flows: Millennium's massive Q4 reversals in AMZN (+398%) and NVDA (+93%) suggest these funds may be reducing risk into earnings, explaining some of the index-level hedging demand we're observing.

Layer 1: Sentiment +52Layer 2: GEX +$628MLayer 3: P/C divergence 0.83Layer 9: FOMC + earnings cluster
OKLOBULLISH
Nuclear momentum continuation
OKLO call volume exploded +939% with 0.21 P/C ratio, building on the small modular reactor theme that's driven the stock higher. The call-heavy flow suggests continued speculative interest in nuclear power infrastructure plays, particularly as AI data center power demand remains a key narrative.
L3: +939% call volL3: P/C 0.21L5: sector momentum
May/Jun call spreads targeting further upside
TXNVOL PLAY
Cheap vol rich skew
TXN presents a classic vol surface anomaly trading -40.5% cheap to sector ETF while maintaining rich skew. This disconnect often precedes significant moves as the market has priced elevated tail risk but compressed overall vol levels, creating potential for sharp reversals.
L4: -40.5% vs sectorL4: cheap vol/rich skewL5: technical neutral
Long straddles targeting vol expansion
LHVOL PLAY
M&A speculation surge
Laboratory Corp saw extreme two-way flow with +26,793% put volume and +12,757% call volume, suggesting merger arbitrage or takeover speculation rather than directional positioning. This explosive vol demand pattern typically emerges around corporate action rumors.
L3: +26,793% putsL3: +12,757% callsL8: potential M&A catalyst
Volatility straddles capturing event premium
